highlights.text: I wrote at the close yesterday that: “My general lean is always to defer to the trend.
My lean is we work to 6794 to backtest where we broke down today, perhaps extend to 6805.” This morning, ES got to 6806 high of day.
It was at this point that the tariff “tape bomb” hit the market, broke down the range, and triggered - for the first time in what felt like weeks - my Bear Case.

I wrote yesterday: “Bear case tomorrow: Bear case tomorrow: No bear case until sub 6712-16 (megaphone failure) but there is a short-term bear case on the failure of the range at 6757 now.” Down we went after, for what ended up being the largest sell since April.
I warned about this on Monday at 4pm when I stated: “Short-term ES is in a multi-day consolidation now mostly 6750 to 6800, with 6765 being a key magnet in the middle.
6757 to 6805-07 remains a massive flag with 6766 being a big magnet in the middle.

post_title: A Tariff "Tape Bomb" Creates The Biggest Red Day Since April For SPX. Can Bulls Buy It? Oct 13 Plan
